steps.title: Set up the equation from the coverage rate
steps.content:
One gallon of paint covers 220 square feet. Since the room's wall area is $w$ square feet and we paint it twice, the total area to paint is $2w$ square feet. Gallons needed equals total area divided by coverage per gallon:
$$P = \frac{2w}{220}$$

steps.title: Simplify and match to the answer choice
steps.content:
Simplify the fraction by canceling the factor of 2:
$$P = \frac{2w}{220} = \frac{w}{110}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
